nodes.label: Conversation Transaction Protocol
nodes.rationale: conversation_id is stable for a conversation's lifetime; turn_id keyed by (conversation_id, idempotency_key) so retries reuse the same turn and user message rather than duplicating; attempt_id tracks one execution try with exactly one terminal state. Storage writes happen before the terminal ChatEvent is sent so the frontend never observes a 'done'/'error' for an uncommitted outcome.

nodes.label: providers.rs PLAT-01 deferral
nodes.rationale: Provider status/switching IPC commands are deferred because no roadmap phase is scheduled for PLAT-01 (local inference provider switching). Module is intentionally unregistered: no #[tauri::command], no capability/permission entry, no command_policy entry, kept as a no-op placeholder.

nodes.label: Deny-by-inventory capability pattern
nodes.rationale: Only commands listed in the capability file are callable from the main window; any command not listed is blocked by default, providing defense-in-depth alongside backend window-label checks.

nodes.label: Metadata-only inspection invariant (no content read before budget check)
nodes.norm_label: metadata-only inspection invariant (no content read before budget check)

nodes.label: Evidence-Gated Memory Engine deterministic replay harness (Phase 1 shadow mode)
nodes.norm_label: evidence-gated memory engine deterministic replay harness (phase 1 shadow mode)

nodes.label: Content Security Policy (default-src self, connect-src ipc)
nodes.norm_label: content security policy (default-src self, connect-src ipc)

nodes.label: Backend-owned credentials policy (D-10): frontend never constructs api_key
nodes.norm_label: backend-owned credentials policy (d-10): frontend never constructs api_key

nodes.label: Strictly increasing sequence per attempt stream protocol invariant
nodes.norm_label: strictly increasing sequence per attempt stream protocol invariant

nodes.label: D-05: hybrid loading UX (skeleton to streaming-content transition)
nodes.norm_label: d-05: hybrid loading ux (skeleton to streaming-content transition)

nodes.label: T-03-16: render FTS5 snippet markers as plain text, not @html, to prevent stored-content XSS
nodes.norm_label: t-03-16: render fts5 snippet markers as plain text, not @html, to prevent stored-content xss

nodes.label: T-03-18: clear debounce timer on unmount to prevent post-unmount invocation
nodes.norm_label: t-03-18: clear debounce timer on unmount to prevent post-unmount invocation

nodes.label: sandboxed iframe with srcdoc preview (empty sandbox attribute)
nodes.norm_label: sandboxed iframe with srcdoc preview (empty sandbox attribute)

nodes.label: D-10: history selection hydrates chatStore then navigates to Chat surface
nodes.norm_label: d-10: history selection hydrates chatstore then navigates to chat surface

nodes.label: Conversation Transaction Protocol
nodes.rationale: conversation_id is learned from the backend Ack event on first send without one and stabilized into historyStore so later sends reuse it; each send/retry carries a client-generated idempotencyKey, with retryMessage reusing the original key so the backend never inserts a duplicate user message; hydrate() replaces the message list with already-persisted backend data and never re-sends those messages. Known limitation: messages loaded via hydrate() have no known idempotencyKey, so retryMessage is a no-op for them.

nodes.label: Agent Issue Protocol
nodes.rationale: Shared structural contract (forensic evidence trace, state delta assertion, blast radius definition, reproduction sequence, execution directive, traceability footer) applied across all agent-filed issue templates to keep multi-agent issue filing deterministic and synchronizable.

nodes.label: FSV-Style State Delta Assertion
nodes.rationale: Physical/Source-of-Truth State Verification pattern: declare Expected vs Observed state to support FSV interrogation of Source of Truth vs returned/observed state, used consistently across bug, anomaly, architecture, dead-code, security, tech-debt, and test-gap templates.

nodes.label: Delete, Don't Comment directive
nodes.rationale: Dead code should be physically deleted rather than commented out, enforced via a verification gate confirming the code is not implicitly invoked through reflection, DI, dynamic dispatch, generated SDKs, framework magic, routing conventions, or runtime registration before deletion.

nodes.label: Credential Advisory Gate / Security Advisory bypass
nodes.rationale: Active leaked credential-class findings must bypass the standard public Security Finding issue template and instead use GitHub Security Advisories so disclosure stays explicitly gated, preventing public exposure of exploitable secrets.
nodes.norm_label: credential advisory gate / security advisory bypass

nodes.label: PR Evidence Contract Validation
nodes.rationale: Validates PR bodies contain substantive, non-placeholder content in required sections (security/release/migration/default profiles) so compilation alone is never treated as sufficient evidence for merge.

nodes.label: Core Value: Privacy-First Local History with Routed Inference
nodes.rationale: Keep local history, files, and agent state private while safely routing AI inference, streaming, and artifacts through explicit backend boundaries.
nodes.norm_label: core value: privacy-first local history with routed inference

nodes.label: Least-Privilege Architecture Decision
nodes.rationale: Architecture intentionally least-privilege: keeps conversation history, attachments, and operational state local while brokering model access through explicit provider routing and hardened preview surfaces.

nodes.label: SHELL-01: Launch app and reach main workspace
nodes.norm_label: shell-01: launch app and reach main workspace

nodes.label: ROUTE-02: Ordered streamed output with cancellation
nodes.norm_label: route-02: ordered streamed output with cancellation

nodes.label: SEC-03: Redact sensitive data before logs/telemetry
nodes.norm_label: sec-03: redact sensitive data before logs/telemetry

nodes.label: Deferred: Database Export/Import Backup
nodes.rationale: storage::backup.rs stays a scaffold placeholder; Phase 3 decided this is user-facing backup/export, not WAL-based crash recovery which SQLite already handles automatically. No v1 requirement calls for it.

nodes.label: Deferred: Richer Streaming-Protocol Contract
nodes.rationale: Per-stream attempt_id/sequence/capability-request-partial hashing, multi-provider fallback policy, resumable retry tokens not implemented. Current ChatEvent contract (Ack/Delta/Done/Error/ArtifactReady plus chat_cancel) satisfies ROUTE-02 with a single hardcoded provider (OpenRouter) and no fallback path; richer contract only earns complexity once a second provider or fallback/resume policy is introduced.

nodes.label: Layered Tauri/Svelte Desktop Client Pattern
nodes.rationale: Backend owns all persistence, provider credentials, file authority, and routing decisions; renderer treated as potentially hostile surface with no secrets, raw paths, or SQL crossing the IPC boundary.

nodes.label: Single Command Policy Authority Pattern
nodes.rationale: Every IPC command validates window label and command-name membership through security::command_policy::policy_check before doing any work, replacing prior per-module duplicated checks.

nodes.label: Six-Source Command Inventory Reconciliation
nodes.rationale: ipc::inventory::verify_inventory() cross-checks command set against command-inventory.toml, registered handlers, permission files, capability files, release capabilities, and command_policy's own table to prevent drift.

nodes.label: Opaque File Token Pattern
nodes.rationale: Raw file paths never exposed to renderer; security::file_tokens mints opaque tokens and the raw path stays backend-owned in AppState.file_tokens.

nodes.label: Lock Ordering Invariant (shell before sqlite)
nodes.rationale: AppState fields guarded by Mutex<T>; shell lock must be acquired before sqlite lock (enforced in get_active_surface) to prevent deadlock across all callers.

nodes.label: Documentation Drift Issue (issue #4)
nodes.rationale: ARCHITECTURE.md and CONCERNS.md previously (2026-06-13) understated implemented scope, describing ipc/providers/security/storage/telemetry as stub-only; by 2026-06-18 main.rs registers 15 commands and chat.rs/inventory.rs are fully implemented. Docs should be refreshed alongside roadmap phase completions.

nodes.label: normalizeIpcError Duplication Across 5 Stores (issue #6)
nodes.rationale: Each store (chat.ts, surface.ts, history.ts, artifacts.ts, settings.ts) defines its own copy of normalizeIpcError; fix is to extract one shared implementation.

nodes.label: Migrations as Embedded Rust Constants (open question)
nodes.rationale: All 4 applied migrations live as &'static str constants in migrations.rs rather than discrete .sql files; flagged before Phase 3 shipped and remains unresolved.
nodes.norm_label: migrations as embedded rust constants (open question)

nodes.label: ipc::providers.rs Unregistered Stub (issue #5)
nodes.rationale: Declared as pub mod but contains no command and never registered in generate_handler!; no requirement currently names a providers status IPC surface.

nodes.label: AppShell.svelte Dead Code
nodes.rationale: No remaining imports anywhere in src/; +page.svelte imports WorkspaceShell.svelte instead. The double-rail risk (CR-01) is dormant since nothing imports it, but the file was never deleted.

nodes.label: GTK/WebKit Toolchain Requirement Gap
nodes.rationale: cargo check/test for src-tauri requires gdk-3.0/webkit2gtk-4.1 system libraries; environments without these cannot compile or test the Rust side, affecting verification rigor on PRs #2 and #7.

nodes.label: Svelte Store Factory Pattern
nodes.rationale: Stores are factory functions returning plain objects with reactive getters, using $state runes instead of Svelte 4 writable/readable.

nodes.label: Optimistic Update with Rollback Pattern
nodes.rationale: Stores apply an optimistic update before the IPC call, then roll back on failure to keep UI responsive while staying consistent with backend state.

nodes.label: In-Memory SQLite Test Helper Pattern
nodes.rationale: Tests create an in-memory Connection directly and use SqlitePool::from_connection to wrap it, keeping unit tests independent of migration ordering.

nodes.label: ShellPreferenceStore UPSERT Decision
nodes.rationale: ShellPreferenceStore uses UPSERT (INSERT ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE) to avoid race between insert/update.

nodes.label: SAVEPOINT-based Migration Runner Decision
nodes.rationale: Migration runner uses SAVEPOINTs not outer transactions because SQLite forbids DDL in some transaction modes.

nodes.label: 01-02-PLAN: Accessible Workspace Shell and Smoke Coverage
nodes.norm_label: 01-02-plan: accessible workspace shell and smoke coverage

nodes.label: Roving Tabindex ARIA Pattern Decision
nodes.rationale: Used ARIA tablist/tab/tabpanel pattern (not nav/button) for the surface rail to correctly express the tab-panel relationship in the assistive technology tree; roving tabindex follows ARIA APG recommendations for composite widgets.

nodes.label: Cargo Integration Test Location Decision
nodes.rationale: Placed cargo integration tests at src-tauri/tests/ (not top-level tests/rust/) because Cargo only discovers integration tests inside the crate under test.

nodes.label: 01-03-PLAN: Bootstrap Wiring and Correctness Fixes (Gap Closure)
nodes.norm_label: 01-03-plan: bootstrap wiring and correctness fixes (gap closure)

nodes.label: CR-01: Missing .setup() Hook
nodes.rationale: main.rs lacked a .setup() hook to open SqlitePool, run migrations, and register managed state, leaving the backend persistence chain structurally disconnected from the IPC layer.

nodes.label: CR-02: Missing Capability Permissions
nodes.rationale: App-defined Tauri 2 commands are deny-by-default and need declared permissions; get_active_surface and set_active_surface had no permission entries, blocking IPC despite correct backend code.

nodes.label: CR-04: SqlitePool::open() Migrations Contract Violation
nodes.rationale: Doc-comment on SqlitePool::open falsely claimed migrations were applied before returning, but the implementation never called run_migrations.

nodes.label: Arc-Shared SqlitePool Decision
nodes.rationale: Arc<SqlitePool> shared between managed SqlitePool and ShellPreferenceStore to avoid opening two connections.

nodes.label: Bare Permission Identifier Convention Decision
nodes.rationale: Bare permission identifiers (allow-get-active-surface) used in capabilities/main.json per Tauri 2 app-own command convention.

nodes.label: Fix: Remove AppShell from Layout (Double Rail)
nodes.rationale: Removed AppShell wrapper which caused a double SurfaceRail in the DOM; layout now solely handles hydration and WorkspaceShell is the complete shell.
nodes.norm_label: fix: remove appshell from layout (double rail)

nodes.label: Fix: Eliminate Race Condition in get_active_surface
nodes.rationale: Held the shell mutex lock for the entire check-read-write hydration sequence so concurrent async invocations can no longer race to produce a stale return value.
nodes.norm_label: fix: eliminate race condition in get_active_surface

nodes.label: Fix: Migration ID Validation Safety Comment
nodes.rationale: Added debug_assert! validating migration ID is alphanumeric/underscore-only plus a SAFETY comment, since migration.id and migration.sql are static literals and this pattern must not be generalized to dynamic values.
nodes.norm_label: fix: migration id validation safety comment

nodes.label: Fix: Mutex Poison Propagates as Panic
nodes.rationale: Mutex poison after a thread panic is unrecoverable; mapping it to rusqlite::Error::InvalidParameterName misclassified a fatal concurrency failure as input validation failure.
nodes.norm_label: fix: mutex poison propagates as panic

nodes.label: CR-01: Double SurfaceRail Render
nodes.rationale: +layout.svelte renders AppShell (which renders SurfaceRail) while +page.svelte renders WorkspaceShell (which also renders SurfaceRail), producing two navigation rails, two divergent focusedIndex states, and duplicate ARIA tablist landmarks.

nodes.label: CR-02: Svelte 4 slot vs Svelte 5 snippet Mismatch
nodes.rationale: +layout.svelte used <slot /> (Svelte 4 API) while the rest of the codebase used {@render children?.()}; mixing these is undefined in Svelte 5 and results in a blank page body.
nodes.norm_label: cr-02: svelte 4 slot vs svelte 5 snippet mismatch

nodes.label: CR-03: Race Condition in get_active_surface
nodes.rationale: Function read (current, hydrated) under one lock acquisition then released it; concurrent async invocations could both pass the !hydrated guard, causing duplicate DB reads and potential stale returns.

nodes.label: CR-04: SQL Injection Precedent in Migration Savepoint
nodes.rationale: Savepoint name and migration SQL assembled via format! string interpolation; not exploitable today (static constants) but sets a precedent future contributors might copy with dynamic content.
nodes.norm_label: cr-04: sql injection precedent in migration savepoint

nodes.label: WR-01: Duplicate schema_migrations Migration
nodes.rationale: Inline bootstrap creates schema_migrations table, then MIGRATIONS slice 0001 repeats the same DDL, creating a confusing dual-creation path for incident diagnosis.

nodes.label: IN-01: AppShell.svelte Dead/Superseded Component
nodes.rationale: AppShell.svelte is a strict subset of WorkspaceShell.svelte; leaving it in the tree risks future contributors reintroducing the double-rail bug.

nodes.label: IN-02: SURFACE_LABELS Duplicated Across Three Files
nodes.rationale: Surface-to-string label mapping defined identically in surface.ts, WorkspaceShell.svelte, and +page.svelte, a future maintenance hazard if a new surface is added.
nodes.norm_label: in-02: surface_labels duplicated across three files

nodes.label: Phase 04 Privacy
nodes.rationale: Phase 4 is the privacy boundary for secrets, file access, redaction, command policy, audit logging, and a minimal settings UI.

nodes.label: Phase 05 Artifacts
nodes.rationale: Phase 5 consumes opaque file tokens from Phase 4 and adds backend-owned artifact preview, persistence, and navigation.

nodes.label: KeyringSecretStore
nodes.rationale: Use OS keychain storage; env-var fallback stays dev/test only and production fails closed.

nodes.label: command_policy
nodes.rationale: Static allow-table policy_check gates all new Phase 4 IPC before work.

nodes.label: file_tokens
nodes.rationale: Backend mints UUID tokens from native picker paths; tokens are session-scoped and in-memory.

nodes.label: Redaction Pipeline
nodes.rationale: Secrets, paths, and content-bearing data are redacted before any log or telemetry write.

nodes.label: Audit Log
nodes.rationale: Append-only JSON Lines audit log writes metadata only after redaction.

nodes.label: SettingsSurface
nodes.rationale: Minimal credential-management UI writes the API key once, shows status, and clears the input after submit.

nodes.label: Tauri Command Surface Registration
nodes.rationale: All new Phase 4 commands are registered in generate_handler and granted in capabilities.

nodes.label: Artifact Sandbox
nodes.rationale: Backend sanitizes artifact HTML, SVG, text, and code and wraps previews in a strict CSP.

nodes.label: Artifact Store
nodes.rationale: Artifact rows are persisted backend-side and re-served as sanitized preview data.

nodes.label: ArtifactReady
nodes.rationale: ArtifactReady is emitted after chat completion and drives artifact navigation.

nodes.label: ArtifactsSurface
nodes.rationale: Host chrome controls preview lifecycle outside the iframe sandbox.

nodes.label: ChatSurface navigation
nodes.rationale: Chat shows a View artifact affordance that routes into the artifacts surface.

nodes.label: Tauri/Svelte AI App Architecture (Adversarial Hardened v5)
nodes.norm_label: tauri/svelte ai app architecture (adversarial hardened v5)

nodes.label: WAL and Write Strategy / Checkpoint Policy
nodes.norm_label: wal and write strategy / checkpoint policy

nodes.label: Give the agent one job at a time
nodes.norm_label: give the agent one job at a time

nodes.label: Fixture Families (adversarial-sse, provider-drift, fts-query-abuse, srcdoc-escaping, wal-recovery, capability-drift)
nodes.norm_label: fixture families (adversarial-sse, provider-drift, fts-query-abuse, srcdoc-escaping, wal-recovery, capability-drift)
